title: Sour Crisp Korean Seaweed Stir-fry
description: A meal-prep friendly korean dish featuring a blend of seaweed, bread rolls, and tandoor for an unexpected yet delicious savory crunch.
instructions: Combine seaweed with the Korean Chili Sauce and Soy Sauce in a bowl, set aside.
Soak bread rolls in water for about 5 minutes. Drain and place on skewers.
Heat a pan over medium heat and add chicken pieces seasoned with salt and pepper. Sear until golden brown, then finish cooking.
Combine cornstarch and water to make a slurry, mix into the seaweed mixture and bring to simmering point, stirring continuously.
Saute garlic and ginger in the same pan, adding green onions last.
Plate up bread rolls with seared chicken. Serve the seaweed sauce on the side or mix it into the chicken for extra tanginess.
